name: Trehalose T2D autophagic-flux trial is recruiting with no posted results
summary: NCT05593549 is direct mechanistic autophagic-flux registry context, but it is recruiting with no posted results and remains disease-context evidence.
statement: ClinicalTrials.gov lists NCT05593549 as a recruiting 60-participant trehalose trial in type 2 diabetes and healthy-aging context with nitric-oxide-mediated vasodilation and autophagic-flux protein-expression primary endpoints and no posted results in the June 11, 2026 API view.
population_or_model: Participants in type 2 diabetes and healthy-aging context
time_horizon: Fourteen-day intervention with estimated study completion on 2029-12-31
quantitative_note: Estimated enrollment is 60; registry status is recruiting; result status is not posted.
caveats: No-results registry status is not evidence of efficacy.
The trial is mechanistic and short, not an aging-function study.
The diabetes microvascular context should not be collapsed into general healthy-aging evidence.
